# DECLuTTER AI — Full LLM Context
# https://github.com/Pastorsimon1798/DECLuTTER-AI

## Detailed Project Information

### Mission
DECLuTTER AI exists because adults with ADHD often feel paralyzed by clutter. Generic organization apps identify objects but don't help users *decide what to do with them*. We convert a messy photo into simple, time-boxed actions with neurodivergent-friendly design.

### Core Principles
1. **Decision over detection**: Finding items is useless without clear next actions
2. **Shame-free language**: No "mess," "junk," "lazy," or "failed" — ever
3. **Energy-aware**: Users choose 2-min, 5-min, 10-min, or listing sprint modes
4. **Privacy by design**: On-device analysis is the default; cloud requires explicit per-group opt-in
5. **Undo everything**: Every decision can be reversed without judgment

### Technical Stack Details

#### Mobile App (Flutter)
- **Language**: Dart 3.5+
- **State Management**: ChangeNotifier / ListenableBuilder (migrating to Riverpod)
- **Navigation**: MaterialPageRoute (migrating to GoRouter)
- **Local Storage**: SharedPreferences (timer persistence); SQLite planned for session history
- **ML Runtime**: ONNX Runtime (primary), TensorFlow Lite (fallback)
- **Camera**: camera plugin with permission_handler
- **Image Processing**: image package for tensor preprocessing
- **Sharing**: share_plus for CSV export

#### Backend (FastAPI)
- **Language**: Python 3.12+
- **Framework**: FastAPI with Pydantic v2
- **Database**: SQLite (MVP); PostgreSQL + SQLAlchemy 2 + Alembic planned
- **AI Provider**: OpenAI Responses API adapter (backend-only; Flutter never calls AI directly)
- **Image Storage**: Google Cloud Storage / S3-compatible (planned)
- **Marketplace**: eBay Sell APIs (OAuth, encrypted tokens)
- **Agent Protocols**: MCP (Model Context Protocol) + A2A (Agent-to-Agent) endpoints
- **Security**: Firebase Auth + App Check, rate limiting, request size limits, structured logging with correlation IDs

### Feature Modules

#### Capture
- Camera preview with focus guidance
- Photo review with retake option
- EXIF stripping for privacy
- Persistent storage of captures

#### Detection
- On-device object detection using quantized models
- Automatic fallback to mock detections when no model present
- Debug overlay with bounding boxes, labels, and confidence scores
- Support for EfficientDet-Lite0 and MobileNet SSD v2

#### Grouping
- Items grouped by category (electronics, books, clothing, furniture, etc.)
- Duplicate detection by class + visual similarity
- Group thumbnails and count display

#### Decision (Decide)
- DecisionCard widget per group
- Five actions: Keep, Donate, Trash, Relocate, Maybe
- Undo stack per group
- Optional note field per decision
- Haptic feedback and semantic labels
- Progress indicator: "X/Y groups decided"

#### Valuation
- Category-based lookup tables (electronics, books, clothing, furniture)
- Condition multipliers (new, good, fair, poor)
- Offline fallback when backend unavailable
- Display: low-mid-high range with confidence

#### Session / Timer
- 10-minute focus sprint timer
- ADHD-friendly quick-start guidance
- Completion celebration and summary
- Session reset for new sprints

#### Summary & Export
- Post-sprint stats (decision counts, total resale value)
- Per-group decision review
- CSV export with proper escaping
- Share sheet integration (mobile) and blob download (web)

#### Backend Endpoints
- `POST /valuation` — simple item valuation
- `POST /valuation/estimate` — comp-based valuation
- `POST /analysis/intake` — image analysis with signed uploads
- `GET /public/listings/{slug}` — shareable public listing pages
- `GET /openapi.json` — OpenAPI spec for agent integration
- `GET /.well-known/agent-card.json` — agent protocol discovery

### Security Features
- Rate limiting (60 req/min global, 10 req/min intake)
- Request size cap (10MB)
- Correlation ID propagation for tracing
- XSS escaping in all HTML outputs
- CSV injection prevention (formula prefixing)
- Path traversal protection in file storage
- Prompt injection validation for storage keys
- Basic/Bearer auth on protected routes
- CORS configuration (no wildcards)

### Testing
- **Flutter**: widget tests for capture, timer, decision card, summary screens
- **Backend**: pytest with 95 tests covering API, security, and edge cases
- **CI**: GitHub Actions for flutter analyze, flutter test, and pytest

### Development Roadmap
1. ✅ Photo capture + on-device detection
2. ✅ Item grouping + decision cards
3. ✅ Valuation + session summary
4. ✅ Security hardening + test coverage
5. 🔄 SQLite persistence for session history
6. 🔄 Riverpod state management migration
7. 🔄 eBay marketplace integration
8. 🔄 AI-powered item analysis (backend)
9. 🔄 Listing draft generation
10. 🔄 Public listing pages + agent endpoints

### Related Projects
- [tflite_flutter](https://github.com/am15h/tflite_flutter) — TensorFlow Lite plugin for Flutter
- [onnxruntime](https://pub.dev/packages/onnxruntime) — ONNX Runtime for Flutter
- [FastAPI](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/) — modern Python web framework

### Citation
If you use or reference DECLuTTER AI in research or publications:
```
Gonzalez de Cruz, S. (2026). DECLuTTER AI: An ADHD-friendly decluttering
assistant with on-device object detection. GitHub repository.
https://github.com/Pastorsimon1798/DECLuTTER-AI
```
